summary: boardflow preinstall downloads and executes remote Windows binary from non-publisher domain
details: On `npm install`, boardflow runs `install.js` via the `preinstall` lifecycle hook. The script is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style string-array shuffling with hex-named identifiers and a `_0x12fa` decoder) and assembles the URL `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe` from concatenated string fragments. It downloads that opaque Windows executable to a randomly-named file in the OS temp directory, chmods it to 0755, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored on Windows, macOS, and Linux. The fetched binary is unpinned, unhashed, and served from a domain unrelated to any declared publisher. The package's stated purpose ("a lightweight kanban board utility for project management workflows") and the bin/boardflow.js kanban CLI are a cover story — the only auto-executed code path on install is the dropper. Any developer or CI machine that runs `npm install boardflow` is compromised by the executed payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
